NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. Set to “The Lilies.” A Testimony of Asaph. A Psalm. Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, You who lead Joseph like a flock; You who dwell between the cherubim, shine forth!
NKJV.2: Before Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh, Stir up Your strength, And come and save us!
NKJV.3: Restore us, O God;  Cause Your face to shine, And we shall be saved!
NKJV.4: O LORD God of hosts,  How long will You be angry Against the prayer of Your people?
NKJV.5: You have fed them with the bread of tears, And given them tears to drink in great measure.
NKJV.6: You have made us a strife to our neighbors, And our enemies laugh among themselves.
NKJV.7: Restore us, O God of hosts; Cause Your face to shine, And we shall be saved!
NKJV.8: You have brought a vine out of Egypt;  You have cast out the nations, and planted it.
NKJV.9: You prepared room for it, And caused it to take deep root, And it filled the land.
NKJV.10: The hills were covered with its shadow, And the mighty cedars with its boughs.
NKJV.11: She sent out her boughs to the Sea, And her branches to the River.
NKJV.12: Why have You broken down her hedges, So that all who pass by the way pluck her fruit?
NKJV.13: The boar out of the woods uproots it, And the wild beast of the field devours it.
NKJV.14: Return, we beseech You, O God of hosts;  Look down from heaven and see, And visit this vine
NKJV.15: And the vineyard which Your right hand has planted, And the branch that You made strong for Yourself.
NKJV.16: It is burned with fire, it is cut down; They perish at the rebuke of Your countenance.
NKJV.17: Let Your hand be upon the man of Your right hand, Upon the son of man whom You made strong for Yourself.
NKJV.18: Then we will not turn back from You; Revive us, and we will call upon Your name.
NKJV.19: Restore us, O LORD God of hosts; Cause Your face to shine, And we shall be saved!

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Shoshannimeduth<S>7802</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of Asaph<S>623</S>. Give ear<S>238</S>, O Shepherd<S>7462</S> of Israel<S>3478</S>, thou that leadest<S>5090</S> Joseph<S>3130</S> like a flock<S>6629</S>; thou that dwellest<S>3427</S> between the cherubims<S>3742</S>, shine forth<S>3313</S>. <sup>of: or, for</sup>
KJV.2: Before<S>6440</S> Ephraim<S>669</S> and Benjamin<S>1144</S> and Manasseh<S>4519</S> stir up<S>5782</S> thy strength<S>1369</S>, and come<S>3212</S> and save<S>3444</S> us. <sup>come: Heb. come for salvation to us</sup>
KJV.3: Turn us again<S>7725</S>, O God<S>430</S>, and cause thy face<S>6440</S> to shine<S>215</S>; and we shall be saved<S>3467</S>.
KJV.4: O LORD<S>3068</S> God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, how long wilt thou be angry<S>6225</S> against the prayer<S>8605</S> of thy people<S>5971</S>? <sup>be: Heb. smoke</sup>
KJV.5: Thou feedest<S>398</S> them with the bread<S>3899</S> of tears<S>1832</S>; and givest them tears<S>1832</S> to drink<S>8248</S> in great measure<S>7991</S>.
KJV.6: Thou makest<S>7760</S> us a strife<S>4066</S> unto our neighbours<S>7934</S>: and our enemies<S>341</S> laugh<S>3932</S> among themselves.
KJV.7: Turn us again<S>7725</S>, O God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, and cause thy face<S>6440</S> to shine<S>215</S>; and we shall be saved<S>3467</S>.
KJV.8: Thou hast brought<S>5265</S> a vine<S>1612</S> out of Egypt<S>4714</S>: thou hast cast out<S>1644</S> the heathen<S>1471</S>, and planted<S>5193</S> it.
KJV.9: Thou preparedst<S>6437</S> room before<S>6440</S> it, and didst cause it to take deep<S>8328</S> root<S>8327</S>, and it filled<S>4390</S> the land<S>776</S>.
KJV.10: The hills<S>2022</S> were covered<S>3680</S> with the shadow<S>6738</S> of it, and the boughs<S>6057</S> thereof were like the goodly<S>410</S> cedars<S>730</S>. <sup>goodly: Heb. cedars of God</sup>
KJV.11: She sent out<S>7971</S> her boughs<S>7105</S> unto the sea<S>3220</S>, and her branches<S>3127</S> unto the river<S>5104</S>.
KJV.12: Why hast thou then broken down<S>6555</S> her hedges<S>1447</S>, so that all they which pass<S>5674</S> by the way<S>1870</S> do pluck<S>717</S> her?
KJV.13: The boar<S>2386</S> out of the wood<S>3293</S> doth waste<S>3765</S> it, and the wild beast<S>2123</S> of the field<S>7704</S> doth devour<S>7462</S> it.
KJV.14: Return<S>7725</S>, we beseech thee, O God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>: look down<S>5027</S> from heaven<S>8064</S>, and behold<S>7200</S>, and visit<S>6485</S> this vine<S>1612</S>;
KJV.15: And the vineyard<S>3657</S> <S>3661</S> which thy right hand<S>3225</S> hath planted<S>5193</S>, and the branch<S>1121</S> that thou madest strong<S>553</S> for thyself.
KJV.16: It is burned<S>8313</S> with fire<S>784</S>, it is cut down<S>3683</S>: they perish<S>6</S> at the rebuke<S>1606</S> of thy countenance<S>6440</S>.
KJV.17: Let thy hand<S>3027</S> be upon the man<S>376</S> of thy right hand<S>3225</S>, upon the son<S>1121</S> of man<S>120</S> whom thou madest strong<S>553</S> for thyself.
KJV.18: So will not we go back<S>5472</S> from thee: quicken<S>2421</S> us, and we will call<S>7121</S> upon thy name<S>8034</S>.
KJV.19: Turn us again<S>7725</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S> God<S>430</S> of hosts<S>6635</S>, cause thy face<S>6440</S> to shine<S>215</S>; and we shall be saved<S>3467</S>.

ESV.1: Give ear, O Shepherd of Israel, you who lead Joseph like a flock. You who are enthroned upon the cherubim, shine forth.
ESV.2: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your might and come to save us!
ESV.3: Restore us,  O God; let your face shine, that we may be saved!
ESV.4: O LORD God of hosts, how long will you be angry with your people's prayers?
ESV.5: You have fed them with the bread of tears and given them tears to drink in full measure.
ESV.6: You make us an object of contention for our neighbors, and our enemies laugh among themselves.
ESV.7: Restore us, O God of hosts; let your face shine, that we may be saved!
ESV.8: You brought a vine out of Egypt; you drove out the nations and planted it.
ESV.9: You cleared the ground for it; it took deep root and filled the land.
ESV.10: The mountains were covered with its shade, the mighty cedars with its branches.
ESV.11: It sent out its branches to the sea and its shoots to the River.
ESV.12: Why then have you broken down its walls, so that all who pass along the way pluck its fruit?
ESV.13: The boar from the forest ravages it, and all that move in the field feed on it.
ESV.14: Turn again, O God of hosts! Look down from heaven, and see; have regard for this vine,
ESV.15: the stock that your right hand planted, and for the son whom you made strong for yourself.
ESV.16: They have burned it with fire; they have cut it down; may they perish at the rebuke of your face!
ESV.17: But let your hand be on the man of your right hand, the son of man whom you have made strong for yourself!
ESV.18: Then we shall not turn back from you; give us life, and we will call upon your name!
ESV.19: Restore us, O LORD God of hosts! Let your face shine, that we may be saved!

NASB.1: For the choir director; [set to] El Shoshannim; Eduth. A Psalm of Asaph. Oh, give ear, Shepherd of Israel, You who lead Joseph like a flock; You who are enthroned [above] the cherubim, shine forth!
NASB.2: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up Your power And come to save us!
NASB.3: O God, restore us And cause Your face to shine [upon us], and we will be saved.
NASB.4: O LORD God [of] hosts, How long will You be angry with the prayer of Your people?
NASB.5: You have fed them with the bread of tears, And You have made them to drink tears in large measure.
NASB.6: You make us an object of contention to our neighbors, And our enemies laugh among themselves.
NASB.7: O God [of] hosts, restore us And cause Your face to shine [upon us], and we will be saved.
NASB.8: You removed a vine from Egypt; You drove out the nations and planted it.
NASB.9: You cleared [the ground] before it, And it took deep root and filled the land.
NASB.10: The mountains were covered with its shadow, And the cedars of God with its boughs.
NASB.11: It was sending out its branches to the sea And its shoots to the River.
NASB.12: Why have You broken down its hedges, So that all who pass [that] way pick its [fruit]?
NASB.13: A boar from the forest eats it away And whatever moves in the field feeds on it.
NASB.14: O God [of] hosts, turn again now, we beseech You; Look down from heaven and see, and take care of this vine,
NASB.15: Even the shoot which Your right hand has planted, And on the son whom You have strengthened for Yourself.
NASB.16: It is burned with fire, it is cut down; They perish at the rebuke of Your countenance.
NASB.17: Let Your hand be upon the man of Your right hand, Upon the son of man whom You made strong for Yourself.
NASB.18: Then we shall not turn back from You; Revive us, and we will call upon Your name.
NASB.19: O LORD God of hosts, restore us; Cause Your face to shine [upon us], and we will be saved.

NLT.1:
Please listen, O Shepherd of Israel,
you who lead Joseph’s descendants like a flock.
O God, enthroned above the cherubim,
display your radiant glory
NLT.2:
to Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh.
Show us your mighty power.
Come to rescue us!
NLT.3:
Turn us again to yourself, O God.
Make your face shine down upon us.
Only then will we be saved.
NLT.4:
O LORD God of Heaven’s Armies,
how long will you be angry with our prayers?
NLT.5:
You have fed us with sorrow
and made us drink tears by the bucketful.
NLT.6:
You have made us the scorn of neighboring nations.
Our enemies treat us as a joke.
NLT.7:
Turn us again to yourself, O God of Heaven’s Armies.
Make your face shine down upon us.
Only then will we be saved.
NLT.8:
You brought us from Egypt like a grapevine;
you drove away the pagan nations and transplanted us into your land.
NLT.9:
You cleared the ground for us,
and we took root and filled the land.
NLT.10:
Our shade covered the mountains;
our branches covered the mighty cedars.
NLT.11:
We spread our branches west to the Mediterranean Sea;
our shoots spread east to the Euphrates River.
NLT.12:
But now, why have you broken down our walls
so that all who pass by may steal our fruit?
NLT.13:
The wild boar from the forest devours it,
and the wild animals feed on it.
NLT.14:
Come back, we beg you, O God of Heaven’s Armies.
Look down from heaven and see our plight.
Take care of this grapevine
NLT.15:
that you yourself have planted,
this son you have raised for yourself.
NLT.16:
For we are chopped up and burned by our enemies.
May they perish at the sight of your frown.
NLT.17:
Strengthen the man you love,
the son of your choice.
NLT.18:
Then we will never abandon you again.
Revive us so we can call on your name once more.
NLT.19:
Turn us again to yourself, O LORD God of Heaven’s Armies.
Make your face shine down upon us.
Only then will we be saved.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “The Lilies of the Covenant.” A Psalm by Asaph. Hear us, Shepherd of Israel, you who lead Joseph like a flock, you who sit above the cherubim, shine out.
WEB.2: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up your might! Come to save us!
WEB.3: Turn us again, God. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.
WEB.4: Yahweh God of Armies, How long will you be angry against the prayer of your people?
WEB.5: You have fed them with the bread of tears, and given them tears to drink in large measure.
WEB.6: You make us a source of contention to our neighbors. Our enemies laugh among themselves.
WEB.7: Turn us again, God of Armies. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.
WEB.8: You brought a vine out of Egypt. You drove out the nations, and planted it.
WEB.9: You cleared the ground for it. It took deep root, and filled the land.
WEB.10: The mountains were covered with its shadow. Its boughs were like God’s cedars.
WEB.11: It sent out its branches to the sea, Its shoots to the River.
WEB.12: Why have you broken down its walls, so that all those who pass by the way pluck it?
WEB.13: The boar out of the wood ravages it. The wild animals of the field feed on it.
WEB.14: Turn again, we beg you, God of Armies. Look down from heaven, and see, and visit this vine,
WEB.15: the stock which your right hand planted, the branch that you made strong for yourself.
WEB.16: It’s burned with fire. It’s cut down. They perish at your rebuke.
WEB.17: Let your hand be on the man of your right hand, on the son of man whom you made strong for yourself.
WEB.18: So we will not turn away from you. Revive us, and we will call on your name.
WEB.19: Turn us again, Yahweh God of Armies. Cause your face to shine, and we will be saved.

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to [the tune of] "Lilies, a Testimony." A Psalm of Asaph. Hear us O Shepherd of Israel,
You who lead Joseph like a flock;
You who sit enthroned above the cherubim [of the ark of the covenant], shine forth!
AMP.2:
Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh, stir up Your power
And come to save us!
AMP.3:
Restore us, O God;
Cause Your face to shine on us [with favor and approval], and we will be saved.
AMP.4:
O LORD God of hosts,
How long will You be angry with the prayers of Your people?
AMP.5:
You have fed them the bread of tears,
And You have made them drink [bitter] tears in abundance.
AMP.6:
You make us an object of contention to our neighbors,
And our enemies laugh [at our suffering] among themselves.
AMP.7:
Restore us, O God of hosts;
And cause Your face to shine on us [with favor and approval], and we will be saved.
AMP.8:
You uprooted a vine (Israel) from Egypt;
You drove out the [Canaanite] nations and planted the vine [in Canaan].
AMP.9:
You cleared away the ground before it,
And it took deep root and filled the land.
AMP.10:
The mountains were covered with its shadow,
And its branches were like the cedars of God.
AMP.11:
Israel sent out its branches to the [Mediterranean] Sea,
And its branches to the [Euphrates] River.
AMP.12:
Why have You broken down its walls and hedges,
So that all who pass by pick its fruit?
AMP.13:
A boar from the woods eats it away,
And the insects of the field feed on it.
AMP.14:
Turn again [in favor to us], O God of hosts;
Look down from heaven and see, and take care of this vine,
AMP.15:
Even the stock which Your right hand has planted,
And [look down on] the son that You have reared and strengthened for Yourself.
AMP.16:
It is burned with fire, it is cut down;
They perish at the rebuke of Your [angry] appearance.
AMP.17:
Let Your hand be upon the man of Your right hand,
Upon the son of man whom You have made strong for Yourself.
AMP.18:
Then we shall not turn back from You;
Revive us and we will call on Your name.
AMP.19:
Restore us, O LORD God of hosts;
Cause Your face to shine on us [in favor and approval], and we shall be saved.

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
Listen, Shepherd, Israel's Shepherd—
get all your Joseph sheep together.
Throw beams of light
from your dazzling throne
MSG.2:
So Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh
can see where they're going.
Get out of bed—you've slept long enough!
Come on the run before it's too late.
MSG.3:
God, come back!
Smile your blessing smile:
That will be our salvation.
MSG.4:
God, God of the angel armies,
how long will you smolder like a sleeping volcano
while your people call for fire and brimstone?
MSG.5:
You put us on a diet of tears,
bucket after bucket of salty tears to drink.
MSG.6:
You make us look ridiculous to our friends;
our enemies poke fun day after day.
MSG.7:
God of the angel armies, come back!
Smile your blessing smile:
That will be our salvation.
MSG.8:
Remember how you brought a young vine from Egypt,
cleared out the brambles and briers
and planted your very own vineyard?
MSG.9:
You prepared the good earth,
you planted her roots deep;
the vineyard filled the land.
MSG.10:
Your vine soared high and shaded the mountains,
even dwarfing the giant cedars.
MSG.11:
Your vine ranged west to the Sea,
east to the River.
MSG.12:
So why do you no longer protect your vine?
Trespassers pick its grapes at will;
MSG.13:
Wild pigs crash through and crush it,
and the mice nibble away at what's left.
MSG.14:
God of the angel armies, turn our way!
Take a good look at what's happened
and attend to this vine.
MSG.15:
Care for what you once tenderly planted—
the vine you raised from a shoot.
MSG.16:
And those who dared to set it on fire—
give them a look that will kill!
MSG.17:
Then take the hand of your once-favorite child,
the child you raised to adulthood.
MSG.18:
We will never turn our back on you;
breathe life into our lungs so we can shout your name!
MSG.19:
God, God of the angel armies, come back!
Smile your blessing smile:
That will be our salvation.

NIV.1:
Psalm 80
For the director of music. To the tune of “The Lilies of the Covenant.” Of Asaph. A psalm.
Hear us, O Shepherd of Israel,
you who lead Joseph like a flock;
you who sit enthroned between the cherubim, shine forth
NIV.2:
before Ephraim, Benjamin and Manasseh.
Awaken your might;
come and save us.
NIV.3:
Restore us, O God;
make your face shine upon us,
that we may be saved.
NIV.4:
O Lord God Almighty,
how long will your anger smolder
against the prayers of your people?
NIV.5:
You have fed them with the bread of tears;
you have made them drink tears by the bowlful.
NIV.6:
You have made us a source of contention to our neighbors,
and our enemies mock us.
NIV.7:
Restore us, O God Almighty;
make your face shine upon us,
that we may be saved.
NIV.8:
You brought a vine out of Egypt;
you drove out the nations and planted it.
NIV.9:
You cleared the ground for it,
and it took root and filled the land.
NIV.10:
The mountains were covered with its shade,
the mighty cedars with its branches.
NIV.11:
It sent out its boughs to the Sea,
its shoots as far as the River.
NIV.12:
Why have you broken down its walls
so that all who pass by pick its grapes?
NIV.13:
Boars from the forest ravage it
and the creatures of the field feed on it.
NIV.14:
Return to us, O God Almighty!
Look down from heaven and see!
Watch over this vine,
NIV.15:
the root your right hand has planted,
the son you have raised up for yourself.
NIV.16:
Your vine is cut down, it is burned with fire;
at your rebuke your people perish.
NIV.17:
Let your hand rest on the man at your right hand,
the son of man you have raised up for yourself.
NIV.18:
Then we will not turn away from you;
revive us, and we will call on your name.
NIV.19:
Restore us, O Lord God Almighty;
make your face shine upon us,
that we may be saved.

NRSVCE.1:

you who lead Joseph like a flock!
You who are enthroned upon the cherubim, shine forth

before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh.

Restore us, O God;

NRSVCE.6:
You make us the scorn of our neighbors;

NRSVCE.7:

NET.1: O shepherd of Israel, pay attention, you who lead Joseph like a flock of sheep! You who sit enthroned above the winged angels, reveal your splendor!
NET.2: In the sight of Ephraim, Benjamin, and Manasseh reveal your power! Come and deliver us!
NET.3: O God, restore us! Smile on us! Then we will be delivered!
NET.4: O LORD God, invincible warrior! How long will you remain angry at your people while they pray to you?
NET.5: You have given them tears as food; you have made them drink tears by the measure.
NET.6: You have made our neighbors dislike us, and our enemies insult us.
NET.7: O God, invincible warrior, restore us! Smile on us! Then we will be delivered!
NET.8: You uprooted a vine from Egypt; you drove out nations and transplanted it.
NET.9: You cleared the ground for it; it took root, and filled the land.
NET.10: The mountains were covered by its shadow, the highest cedars by its branches.
NET.11: Its branches reached the Mediterranean Sea, and its shoots the Euphrates River.
NET.12: Why did you break down its walls, so that all who pass by pluck its fruit?
NET.13: The wild boars of the forest ruin it; the insects of the field feed on it.
NET.14: O God, invincible warrior, come back! Look down from heaven and take notice! Take care of this vine,
NET.15: the root your right hand planted, the shoot you made to grow!
NET.16: It is burned and cut down. They die because you are displeased with them.
NET.17: May you give support to the one you have chosen, to the one whom you raised up for yourself!
NET.18: Then we will not turn away from you. Revive us and we will pray to you!
NET.19: O LORD God, invincible warrior, restore us! Smile on us! Then we will be delivered!
